Text: Always prioritize clinic flow if unsure what to do.
Rooming flow is the highest operational priority; protect at least a 1:2 rooming ratio whenever mathematically possible.
At least one PSR/front desk function must be covered. If no PSR is assigned, the Office Manager acts as PSR/front desk coverage.
If help is needed, the person needing help should ask directly, in person when possible, and verify that the request was heard.
Rooming means the patient-flow work from the beginning of rooming until the patient is leaving for checkout, including nurse visits when applicable.
RNs listed in the staffing selector are RNs acting as RNs. If an RN shifts to rooming, that RN is not counted as dedicated RN coverage for priority logic.
Some PSRs may be MA/PSR cross-trained and able to room patients. The playbook notes this only when shifting that person affects minimum staffing priorities.
